fields.Name: Nimble AMS
fields.Description: Nimble AMS connector — Salesforce-native AMS; data lives as SF sObjects (standard + NU__ managed-package customs). Surfaces: Nimble Fuse (NUINT inbound/outbound), Salesforce REST (sObject CRUD, SOQL, describe), and NAMS LMS REST.

OutboundIntegration.description: Named SOQL read: caller POSTs a named integration-setting with optional <<param>> substitutions; Nimble Fuse executes the configured SOQL query and returns records.
OutboundIntegration.limits: 50000 records OR 3MB per call — whichever is reached first
OutboundIntegration.pagination: None built-in. Pagination must be implemented via SOQL WHERE clause offsets in the admin-configured query.

InboundIntegration.description: External-ID-keyed JSON upsert: caller POSTs records in InboundRecords format; Nimble Fuse upserts via the configured External ID field. Batch size max 10,000 (recommended 5,000).

Configuration.TokenRefreshStrategy: Salesforce OAuth2 refresh_token grant: POST https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token with grant_type=refresh_token. Access tokens expire; refresh token must be persisted and re-used.

PaginationDefaults.FuseAPI: None — no built-in pagination. Use SOQL WHERE clause with filters.
PaginationDefaults.SalesforceSOQL: Cursor — Salesforce REST SOQL returns nextRecordsUrl when results exceed 2000 (default) or up to 2000 per page. Follow nextRecordsUrl until absent.

Configuration.IncrementalSyncApproach: LastModifiedDate SOQL filter in outbound integration settings. Watermark field is LastModifiedDate (datetime ISO8601). Alternatively Salesforce CDC (Change Data Capture) via Platform Events streaming — org-dependent.
Configuration.WebhooksNote: Nimble AMS has no native webhook system. Salesforce Platform Events / CDC can be used for event-driven patterns but requires org configuration and a separate streaming subscription (not standard REST polling).
Configuration.BulkOperationsNote: Nimble Fuse inbound supports batches up to 10,000 records per call. The Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 (/jobs/ingest) is a separate surface not exposed via Nimble Fuse — it requires direct Salesforce REST API credentials and is outside the Nimble AMS connector scope.
Configuration.APIVersioningStrategy: URL-path — Salesforce REST API version is embedded in the path: /services/data/v{version}/ (e.g. v59.0). The Fuse endpoint (/services/apexrest/NUINT/NUIntegrationService) is version-agnostic.

ErrorResponseShape.FuseAPI: HTTP 4xx/5xx with JSON body. On field errors, Message field contains Salesforce exception text. On batch processing, InboundResults array contains per-record Success=false + ErrorMessage per failed record.
ErrorResponseShape.SalesforceREST: [{errorCode: string, message: string, fields: string[]}] — array of error objects with Salesforce error codes.

Configuration.CustomObjectMarkerPattern: __c suffix on sObject API name (e.g. NU__Membership__c). Standard Salesforce custom object naming.
Configuration.CustomFieldMarkerPattern: __c suffix on field API name. Standard Salesforce custom field naming.
Configuration.universalPKNote: Salesforce 18-character case-insensitive record ID. Present on every sObject as a read-only system field. 15-char IDs are also valid (case-sensitive) — always store as 18-char.

WriteCapability.Delete: Supported via Salesforce REST API sObject DELETE, NOT via Nimble Fuse. Fuse inbound is upsert-only.
WriteCapability.Note: Nimble Fuse inbound supports External-ID-keyed upsert (create + update). Hard deletes require direct Salesforce REST API calls (DELETE /services/data/vXX.0/sobjects/{SObjectType}/{id}).

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNote: Nimble Fuse API has no ETag, If-Match, or version field in its request/response contract. Direct Salesforce sObject REST API supports ETag via If-Match header (see Salesforce connector), but this path is not used by Nimble Fuse inbound.
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNote: Salesforce DELETE removes the record to the org's recycle bin (queryable via isDeleted=true for up to 15 days) then permanently purges. The connector cannot distinguish recycle-bin from permanent delete without querying deletedRecords endpoint. Treat as hard delete for sync purposes.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionNote: ConcurrencyControl=none means no ETag/version for optimistic locking. DestWins (latest write to MJ wins) is the safe default. MostRecent not recommended without server-side versioning.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.family: Salesforce platform standard objects beyond Nimble AMS managed package
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: Generic Salesforce breadth (e.g. Opportunity, Lead, Case, standard platform objects not part of the NU__ managed package) is not in scope for the Nimble AMS connector. These are accessible via Salesforce REST API but are not Nimble AMS-specific.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.family: Per-org custom schema (customer-defined custom fields and objects)
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: Customer-specific customizations (fields/objects added by the association's Salesforce admin) are not in the Nimble managed package and cannot be declared as static Declared metadata. They are runtime-Discovered via Salesforce sObject describe at connection creation time.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.family: Salesforce Tooling, Metadata, Analytics, Knowledge APIs
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: These Salesforce-platform APIs are not part of the Nimble AMS integration surface. The connector scopes to data objects (NU__ sObjects and standard SF objects used by AMS), not platform administration APIs.
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.accessIfNeeded: Not in scope for Nimble AMS connector.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: Callout type triggers third-party APIs FROM Nimble AMS (Nimble calls external systems). This is an outbound-webhook direction not relevant to the MJ connector's inbound-read direction.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.family: Apex framework objects (FuseInboundRecord, FuseOutboundRecord, PaymentAPI, CartPricingFramework, TriggerFramework, UiApi)
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: These are Apex infrastructure classes documented in nams-api-docs, not syncable sObjects with data. They are Salesforce developer extension points, not integration data surfaces.

Payment processing requires the Nimble Payment API (CreditCardDetail, CheckDetail, etc.) which is an Apex-only surface. Creating/modifying payment records via simple Fuse inbound upsert is not supported and risks data integrity. This family should be Pull-only.
OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.accessIfNeeded: Read-only via Salesforce REST SOQL. Writes require Apex payment processing classes.

Configuration.OutboundResponseNullOmissionNote: Outbound integration responses omit fields with null values. Parsers must treat missing fields as nullable (not as schema errors). This is an explicit Nimble Fuse behavior documented in the source docs.
Configuration.InstanceURLNote: The Salesforce org instance URL is org-specific (e.g. https://na1.salesforce.com or https://myorg.my.salesforce.com). It is obtained from the OAuth2 token response (instance_url field) and must be persisted per-org. MyDomain orgs use *.my.salesforce.com.

ProbeVerdicts.note: RealityProbe marked these 3 apexrest paths "wrong" on HTTP 400. Re-probe (read-only, unauthenticated) proved the 400 is uniform Salesforce auth-gating (URL_NOT_RESET), NOT path-validation: a deliberately-fake path AND the canonical /services/data/v59.0/ return the identical 400 on the only credential-free Salesforce host (login.salesforce.com). The declared paths are docs-verbatim and correct; the verdicts resolve as probe artifacts. Real path validation requires an authenticated per-org instance_url.

resolved.rationale: 400 URL_NOT_RESET is uniform auth-gating (identical to canonical /services/data/ control); host cannot validate path existence credential-free. Declared path is docs-verbatim and correct. Probe 400 is an artifact, not a falsification.

400 URL_NOT_RESET is uniform auth-gating (identical to canonical /services/data/ control); host cannot validate path existence credential-free. Declared path is docs-verbatim and correct. Probe 400 is an artifact, not a falsification.

400 URL_NOT_RESET is uniform auth-gating (identical to canonical /services/data/ control); host cannot validate path existence credential-free. Declared path is docs-verbatim and correct. Probe 400 is an artifact, not a falsification.

fields.Name: FuseEndpoint
fields.Description: The single Nimble Fuse integration endpoint (/services/apexrest/NUINT/NUIntegrationService). A POST-only Apex REST endpoint that routes to admin-configured inbound (External-ID upsert) or outbound (named SOQL read) integration settings. Not a data record type itself; represents the Fuse contract.

AccessPath.door: NUINT/NUIntegrationService (POST)
AccessPath.note: Direction (inbound upsert vs outbound SOQL read) is selected by the admin-configured integration-setting name in the Request envelope. No pagination.

Configuration.CustomFieldsNote: NU__/custom field schema is runtime-Discovered via Salesforce sObject describe (auth-gated).

fields.Name: Name
fields.Description: Name of the admin-configured integration setting to invoke (selects inbound vs outbound).

fields.Name: Authentication Key
fields.Description: Authentication key (note the space in the JSON key name) configured on the integration setting.

fields.Name: Parameters
fields.Description: Outbound: map of <<param>> substitutions for the configured SOQL query.

fields.Name: InboundRecords
fields.Description: Inbound: array of { Fields: {...} } records to upsert via the configured External ID field.

fields.Name: Records
fields.Description: Outbound response: array of records returned by the configured SOQL query (null fields omitted).

fields.Name: RecordCount
fields.Description: Number of records in the response.

fields.Name: Message
fields.Description: Status / error message; on errors contains the Salesforce exception text.

fields.Name: InboundResults
fields.Description: Inbound response: per-record { ExternalId, SalesforceId, Success, ErrorMessage } results.

fields.Name: Account
fields.Description: Nimble AMS Account (Salesforce sObject Account). Accessed via Salesforce REST SOQL / the Nimble Fuse outbound named-SOQL door. Business-field schema is runtime-Discovered via Salesforce sObject describe (auth-gated); only the universal Salesforce system fields are credential-free-provable and declared here.

AccessPath.door: Salesforce REST SOQL query (/services/data/v{apiVersion}/query) OR Fuse outbound named-SOQL setting
AccessPath.pagination: nextRecordsUrl cursor (Salesforce returns batches of up to 2000; follow nextRecordsUrl until absent)

Configuration.CustomFieldsNote: NU__/custom field schema is runtime-Discovered via Salesforce sObject describe (auth-gated).

fields.Name: Id
fields.Description: Salesforce 18-character case-insensitive record ID. Read-only system field present on every sObject; the universal primary key.

fields.Name: CreatedDate
fields.Description: System datetime the record was created. Read-only universal Salesforce system field.

fields.Name: LastModifiedDate
fields.Description: System datetime of last modification. Read-only universal Salesforce system field; the incremental-sync watermark.

fields.Name: SystemModstamp
fields.Description: System-maintained datetime updated on any system or user modification. Read-only universal Salesforce system field.

fields.Name: IsDeleted
fields.Description: Whether the record has been moved to the recycle bin. Read-only universal Salesforce system field.

fields.Name: LmsProduct
fields.Description: LMS-eligible product (where "Sync with LMS" is checked), exposed via the NAMS LMS REST API. Read-only; supports custom fields via Field Sets.

AccessPath.door: /lms/v1/products (GET)

Configuration.CustomFieldsNote: LMS API exposes org-specific custom fields under a `custom` JSON key via Field Sets (case-1 declared marker; specific fields runtime-determined).

fields.Name: productType
fields.Description: LMS product productType field (from documented example response).

fields.Name: name
fields.Description: LMS product name field (from documented example response).

fields.Name: id
fields.Description: LMS product record ID (Salesforce Product record ID). Addressed via /lms/v1/products/{id}.

fields.Name: description
fields.Description: LMS product description field (from documented example response).

fields.Name: LmsPurchase
fields.Description: LMS purchase (an Order Item Line record) for an LMS product, exposed via the NAMS LMS REST API. Supports sync write-back (completionStatus/syncStatus/externalId) via POST; supports custom fields via Field Sets.

AccessPath.door: /lms/v1/purchases (GET)

Configuration.CustomFieldsNote: LMS API exposes org-specific custom fields under a `custom` JSON key via Field Sets (case-1 declared marker; specific fields runtime-determined).

fields.Name: syncStatus
fields.Description: LMS purchase syncStatus field (writable via the LMS Purchase Sync POST body).

fields.Name: status
fields.Description: LMS purchase status field (writable via the LMS Purchase Sync POST body).

fields.Name: product
fields.Description: Embedded nested LMS product object (access-path, NOT a scalar FK). The Get Purchases response nests product as { productType, name, id, description }; it has no scalar id pointing at a separate LmsProduct row, so it is a 1:N/embedded access-path on the purchase, not a foreign key.

fields.Name: id
fields.Description: LMS purchase record ID (Order Item Line record ID). Addressed via /lms/v1/purchases/{line_id}.

fields.Name: externalId
fields.Description: External system identifier for the purchase (nullable; writable via the LMS Purchase Sync POST body).

fields.Name: customerId
fields.Description: Customer Salesforce record ID who made the purchase (filterable via ?customerId=). References a Salesforce Account/Contact; FK target deferred to runtime (ambiguous which object).

fields.Name: completionStatus
fields.Description: LMS purchase completionStatus field (writable via the LMS Purchase Sync POST body).
